patch.value: Deduplicate item-type definition normalization across settings and registry

Duplicate audit 2026-05-22: the repo static quality gate with --duplicate-window 10 found the command-option-policy normalization block duplicated between src/core/item/type-registry.ts:430-443 and src/core/store/settings.ts:340-353. Manual inspection shows the surrounding normalizeTypeDefinition/normalizeItemTypeDefinition functions also repeat the same name trim, optional folder handling, aliases, required_create_fields, required_create_repeatables, options normalization, command_option_policies dedupe key, and stable sort semantics. Search found pm-e1va for schema/types.json runtime loading and pm-why9 for CLI parser/default-status duplication, but no active item for single-sourcing item-type definition normalization itself. Fix by extracting a shared item-type definition normalizer/factory that both settings persistence and runtime registry consume, with adapters only where each layer truly differs.

Settings and runtime registry use one shared normalization path for ItemTypeDefinition objects; command_option_policies dedupe/sort semantics are tested once and consumed by both callers; behavior stays compatible for blank names, optional aliases, folder trimming, required_create_* fields, options, and policy arrays; focused settings/type-registry tests plus pnpm build pass.

value.author: codex-duplication-audit
value.text: Evidence: duplicate-check searches before creation included 'duplicate duplicated duplication repeated copy-paste code logic tests text', 'Deduplicate', 'item type registry settings default definitions custom item types duplicate', plus reads of pm-e1va and pm-why9. Similar existing items cover schema/types runtime loading and default status/workflow duplication, but not the duplicated ItemTypeDefinition normalization logic in settings.ts and type-registry.ts. Static scan evidence: node scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs --duplicate-window 10 --max-duplicate-chunks 200 --json reported src/core/item/type-registry.ts:434 vs src/core/store/settings.ts:344.

value.author: item-type-norm-dedup
value.text: START: claimed pm-v798. Diffed normalizeTypeDefinition (type-registry.ts:416-459) vs normalizeItemTypeDefinition (settings.ts:323-364). Only genuine layer difference: policy-command handling. Registry validates+lowercases command (untrusted extension/file input, rejects non-create/update); settings keeps the already-validated typed command verbatim. normalizeList === normalizeStringList byte-for-byte; option + policy dedupe/sort identical. Extracting a shared normalizer with an injected ResolvePolicyCommand adapter.

value.author: item-type-norm-dedup
value.text:
DONE (pure dedup, behavior preserved).

FILES:
- NEW src/core/item/item-type-definition.ts (shared pure normalizer)
- NEW tests/unit/item-type-definition.spec.ts (24 cases, 100% cov)
- src/core/item/type-registry.ts: removed local normalizeList/normalizeOptionDefinition/normalizeCommandOptionPolicyDefinition + body of normalizeTypeDefinition; normalizeTypeDefinition is now a thin adapter -> normalizeItemTypeDefinition(def,{resolvePolicyCommand:strictPolicyCommand}); applyTypeDefinitions now uses normalizeItemTypeStringList.
- src/core/store/settings.ts: removed local normalizeItemTypeOptionDefinition/normalizeItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy/normalizeItemTypeDefinition; imports the shared normalizeItemTypeDefinition (default keepPolicyCommand resolver); dropped now-unused ItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy/ItemTypeOptionDefinition type imports.

SHARED MODULE SIGNATURE (src/core/item/item-type-definition.ts):
- normalizeItemTypeStringList(values?: string[]): string[]
- normalizeItemTypeOption(option): ItemTypeOptionDefinition | null
- type ResolvePolicyCommand = (command: string) => ('create'|'update') | null
- keepPolicyCommand: ResolvePolicyCommand (pass-through; settings, validated input)
- strictPolicyCommand: ResolvePolicyCommand (trim+lowercase, reject non-create/update; registry, untrusted input)
- normalizeItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy(policy, resolve): ItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy | null
- normalizeItemTypeCommandOptionPolicies(policies?, resolve): ItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy[] (dedupe `${command}:${option.toLowerCase()}`, stable sort by command then option)
- normalizeItemTypeDefinition(def, {resolvePolicyCommand?=keepPolicyCommand}): ItemTypeDefinition | null

LAYER-SPECIFIC ADAPTERS KEPT: the ResolvePolicyCommand injection is the ONLY divergence. Registry -> strictPolicyCommand; settings -> default keepPolicyCommand. Presence-preservation (set-but-empty vs unset) for folder/aliases/required_create_*/options/command_option_policies is preserved exactly. Registry-only coerceTypeDefinitionFromUnknown/applyTypeDefinitions/resolve* untouched (no duplication there).

VITESS INCLUDE LINE TO ADD: "src/core/item/item-type-definition.ts", after "src/core/item/item-format.ts" (alphabetical neighbor) in vitest.config.ts include list (~line 72).

VERIFY: npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json => EXIT 0. Focused run (item-type-definition.spec + settings-store.spec + settings-validator.spec + item-types-file.spec) => 4 files, 87 tests passed. Coverage of new module via scoped vitest: 100% stmts/branches/funcs/lines (24 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: COORDINATOR VERIFIED 2026-05-24: combined gate green (new module 100% covered), static gate passed. Dogfooded schema add-type Spike with aliases -> settings persisted aliases sorted+deduped (research,spk); create via alias resolved to Spike. Shared normalizer single-sources both registry and settings paths.
